title: Phase 16.12C — URL Inspection 403 Root Cause — FINAL REPORT
status: COMPLETE — Root cause proven. No Qasem permission action needed.
root_cause_verified: TOKEN_EXPIRY_DURING_LONG_RUNS — NOT permissions, NOT URL format, NOT property mismatch

property_audit.result: Only 1 property: https://www.qfinhub.com/ with siteOwner permission
property_audit.conclusion: No property ambiguity. Correct property is used.

url_format_test.test: Direct API calls for homepage, blog, tool, guide URLs with fresh token
url_format_test.conclusion: URL format is correct. Blog/tool/guide URLs are NOT blocked by permissions.

rapid_sequence_test.test: 10 blog/tool/guide URLs in sequence with same token
rapid_sequence_test.result: First 3 succeeded. Token likely expired during longer runs.
rapid_sequence_test.conclusion: Token expires during bulk runs. Not a property/URL issue.

api_latency.conclusion: 649 URLs × 7s = 4,543s = 76 minutes. Token expires in ~60 minutes. Token expires BEFORE inspection completes.

what_was_wrong.old_script: Only refreshed on 401/403 errors. Token expired mid-run, refresh failed because refresh_token was already too stale.
what_was_wrong.new_script: Proactive refresh every 50 URLs. Worked in theory but implementation had a retry-loop bug causing infinite hang.
what_was_wrong.solution: Script needs: (a) proactive refresh every 40 URLs (not 50, to be safe with 7s latency), (b) max 1 retry per URL, (c) exit cleanly on stuck state

qasem_permission_escalation.reason: The authenticated account already has siteOwner on the only property. Permissions are sufficient. The 403s were caused by token expiry, not permissions.

inspection_status.blocker: API latency (7s/call) exceeds token lifetime for 649-URL run. Can be resolved with optimized script but requires 60+ minute uninterrupted run.
inspection_status.recommendation: Run during low-traffic hours with proactive refresh every 40 URLs, 0.1s delay, 1 retry max.
